section_title: INTRODUCTION AND WEEKLY DOLLAR INDEX CHART SETUP
content:
Good evening folks, I hope you're all doing very well. Please confirm on Twitter with a '5x5' that you can
hear me and see my weekly Dollar Index chart. I'm going to ask you to give me a heads up to see how
much time delay there is between me speaking to you all and you giving me a heads up. Thank you,
Alfredo Tavor, for that confirmation.

section_title: PREVIOUS WEEK'S CAUTION AND TRADE CRITERIA
content:
Last weekend, before the market open, I mentioned I was not willing to take anything as a trade unless it
showed me a move on the daily chart with a close above this wick's upper quadrant. You can see we never
even got up there, so Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, there was no interest, no concern. I'm
content with that. I'll show you what was obvious on the NASDAQ that I actually executed on, which you
can see in my Twitter feed. We had an inside week, which means this measurement is no longer
necessary, but I'm still interested to see it try to get above that level.

section_title: CONSEQUENT ENCROACHMENT AND INEFFICIENCY
content:
We have this wick here, and we can measure it. You want to have 105.774, or 105.818 as consequent
encroachment. If we were to have one more drop down to revisit this inefficiency, which you'll see more
plainly when I remove the FIB, that is consequent encroachment of that candlestick's tail or wick. If we do
get some weakness, that would be an area I would watch. I would want to see it support that, but it's better
if it doesn't do it, because I'm trying not to pick a top on the Dollar Index with all the things going on around
the world. I don't think we're done yet with it going higher, but I'm completely comfortable with being proven
wrong. This inefficiency here, with consequent encroachment of this wick, they overlap together, so that's a
convergence between two discount arrays.

section_title: FAIR VALUE GAP (BISI) ANALYSIS
content:
On the daily chart, if you recall from last week's live stream, I mentioned this fair value gap here that's
shaded. I'm going to color it a little bit differently so it really stands out. I said we could draw down into that
level, but I don't think that just because we go down there, I'm not long dollar. Meaning, that would be
shorts forming in Euro and Cable. Go back and listen to the live stream last week. I would still want to see it
go higher first to the upper quadrant of that weekly candlestick's wick that I mentioned before we dropped
down to the daily chart. Notice what happened last week: Friday, Thursday, Wednesday, Tuesday, Monday,
we spent a lot of time down in this gap. Between this candle's high and this candle's low, that's what's being
highlighted. This is a Buy-Side Imbalance Sell-Side Inefficiency (BISI), it's a fair value gap by classification,
but specifically because it's an up-closed candle, that means it's a BISI.

section_title: MARKET CAUTION AND SUNDAY TRADING OUTLOOK
content:
It spent a lot of time in that last week. In the commentary, I mentioned how I was placing a limit on my
interest and filtering how much interest I can have in the marketplace. It had to meet a specific criteria
before I would take a trade. I went the entirety of the week until Friday when I finally saw something that I
was willing to take, but by far and large, my caution last week was rewarded. As you can see, it really
wasn't doing a whole lot in terms of movement. On the 4-hour chart, here's that daily BISI imbalance
efficiency. We spent most of the time with the bodies of the candles respecting halfway or consequent
encroachment of that blue shaded area. They were taking it lower, and this one here went one more time
down into it, and then we're just meandering around. Do we go lower? I don't know if we're going to do that
because it's Sunday. I've mentioned this many times before: Sunday is a gamble. I don't know how to
predict the opening price on the new week, so I have to sit and wait like anybody else and determine what
they do from the opening price. We'll get a new week opening gap; we can see what we see from there
going into Sunday, Monday, London open, New York open, and then how we trade on Monday.

section_title: RANGE-BOUND ACTION AND SMT DIVERGENCE
content:
On the hourly chart, you can see how we were pretty much just going sideways all week. Here's Friday's
trading, Thursday, Wednesday, Tuesday, and Monday. Basically, range-bound. The only thing that was
noteworthy and that you should have in your journal is to take a look at these highs here. If you look at that
in relationship to the Euro Dollar, Euro Dollar failed to make a lower low as the Dollar made this higher high
here. That's SMT Divergence. That was a catalyst for Euro to move slightly higher, and then this took the
sell side out below here, but notice that we didn't get a close below and outside of that blue shaded area,
which is the daily BISI (Buy-Side Imbalance Sell-Side Inefficiency).

section_title: MARKET OPENING EXPECTATIONS AND GEOPOLITICAL SENTIMENT
content:
I want to see a gap lower opening on NASDAQ, on E-mini S&P, and on the Dow futures. By contrast, I
would like to see a gap opening higher on Dollar, maybe come down, try to fill it, then rush to go higher.
That's what I would like to see; it would support the idea of not trying to pick a top on the Dollar. It would
also confirm what I outlined on Friday when I was in my trade, but I don't want to carry anything over the
weekend because of gap risk. I didn't notice a whole lot of things happening overseas that would cause a
lot of excitement. Of course, I don't have the intel that would be in Northern Gaza right now, so whatever is
going on up there is kind of like a media blackout. Whatever may be happening, we may find out later this
week, but all eyes are on the Middle East, and there's a lot of concern about that. That is being factored in,
built in, wrapped up into sentiment for the marketplace, and none of it's favorable for risk going higher. If
anything happens this week that is seen as a rally in risk
- meaning like Euro Dollar, Pound Dollar,
NASDAQ, or any of the indices or stocks
- if they go higher, I think they're going higher to be faded. That
would be my expectation: it would be short-lived and then be met with more shorting.

section_title: DOLLAR CONSOLIDATION AND TRADING EXOTIC FOREX PAIRS
content:
The Dollar Index has to start showing me a little bit more in here, because the fact that it's being
consolidated and held in a range like this isn't as helpful. All that does is allow the crosses
- for instance,
like the Euro Pound and the Aussie New Zealand, those exotic pairs that are not coupled with Dollar
- when
Dollar is in consolidation like that, that's when those unrelated dollar-based Forex pairs are permitted to
move around freely, and they have exaggerated moves. It's based on interest rate differential, so the
stronger will move better than the weaker, and the weaker will move softer than the stronger. When Dollar
is in consolidation, as a Forex Trader, you want to be thinking about, 'Okay, then I need to look at the
relationships between the interest rates.' The interest rates between the two currencies that make up a
pair, if they're paired together and it has to be a pair that's not with the Dollar Index. Since the central banks
are holding Dollar in consolidation, you have to take your attention away from the majors and look at the
Exotics, anything that doesn't have a Dollar associated with the pair. Then, whichever one's stronger versus
the weakest, if you compare those two up, that's where the bigger moves are going to occur. If you look at
last week's trading, I taught this in core content too, it's all on my YouTube channel for free, but I'm just
reminding you all because I know a lot of you are predominantly Forex Traders, and most of my students
are Forex Traders. Don't lose sight of whenever the market is doing this in Dollar: if it's consolidating, your
mind should not be trying to trade those dollar-based currencies, start looking at the Exotic crosses. I
would rather see it open gap higher, trade down, and then rally and start moving for the Buy Side here and
above these highs.

section_title: INSIDE WEEK AND RANGE EXPANSION
content:
Let's take it on into Euro Dollar here. Here's Euro on its weekly chart. In similar fashion, we had an inside
week last week here. An inside week, I mentioned it earlier when I was talking about the Dollar Index, an
inside candle or inside range, inside bar, inside weekly range, means that the week that we closed
- its low,
I'm sorry, its high, it's lower than the previous week's high, and its low is higher than the previous week's
low. That means it's usually an indication that there's going to be a large move very shortly, usually within
the next two candles. I like to anticipate the very next candle, that would be like this current week that we're
opening up here in about 17 minutes or so. When that market opens, or when this weekly market opens, I
would expect the beginning of a large weekly range. Now, we may not get it this week; it may be deferred
one more week, but usually it doesn't make you wait longer than two time frames. Since I'm utilizing the
weekly chart here as I'm discussing this, the expectation would be that we expect to see a large range
weekly candle. Now, by and in of itself, it doesn't give you a directional prognostication; it just tells you the
magnitude of the move. In other words, how much energy should be shown in price delivery, so you're still
required to have some measure of prognostication behind the direction. Are you bullish or are you bearish?

section_title: INSIDE RANGE MECHANICS AND TRADING IMPLICATIONS
content:
If you have an inside range or inside candlestick or bar, it's usually the very next candle or the next candle
after it. So inside candles or inside ranges has a lower high than the previous range and a higher low than
the previous range. All that means is it's compressing, and we expect range expansion, large ranges, to
come to fruition. Now, if we don't get it
- this is for your notes
- if you don't get it after two, like say for
instance we're looking at the weekly chart, if we don't get it this week or next week and it's still range
- bound, then that usually implies that it's going to consolidate longer, to not anticipate a large range move.
So it's kind of like, you know, you got to balance these things out and take from it what you can. But I like to
anticipate the very next candle to be a large range expansion or the very next one. And then if it doesn't
give it to me, then I go right into scalping, and I'm not looking for like a one-shot, one-kill scenario, which is
what I would favor in these conditions where we're expecting a large range move on the weekly. That would
be great for the series of, you know, the daily chart creating one-sidedness predominantly over the course
of the week, so between Monday's trading, Friday's close, I would expect more directional trading given the
context that I'm showing you here. And I've already showed and told you that I favor a higher Dollar, that's
what I favor, but what I see here at the open with you in a couple minutes, I got to get through this, that will
help me decide what I would like to see going into London tonight and then going into New York tomorrow.
So I'll kind of like talk over that as we see what the market's doing at the opening.

section_title: BEARISH BIAS AND GAP SCENARIO
content:
On the daily chart, it was range bound in here. We did come down into this candle right here on
Wednesday and Thursday and just found a little bit of support in here. I'm not terribly bullish; I like I said, I'd
like to see this showing willingness to want a gap lower opening, then trade up, fill it, and then work
towards moving lower. That's what I'd like to see, because I'd like to see it. It doesn't mean that it has to do
it, I'm not trying to impose my will on it, but if it does that, then it gives me a lot of confidence going into
Monday to anticipate further weakness. That's what I would look for. It doesn't mean I'm going to try to
trade on Monday, it just means that that would be supporting the idea that I expected going into Friday's
close last week when I shorted NQ. Now, if I'm wrong, say we gap higher, we trade down, then I would look
for a move in the first half of the week to try to trade up into this area here, and if it fails to do that, then we
would resume weakness. But I don't necessarily see that as something I would trade; I wouldn't go long
based on that. I still favor shorts, and until proven otherwise or incorrect and I'm welcoming that
opportunity to be proven incorrect, you know I want to see evidence that supports the idea that I'm
wrong. And if it gives me that insight, it costs me nothing to get it. Number one, I'm not risking anything
because I'm giving you my expectations technically, but I don't have any hardline risk behind it. So I'm still
in that Intel state where I'm trying to get information and insight from the marketplace risk-free. And then
once I get a determination of what I think it may be trying to do this week that's high probability, then and
only then I'd be willing to take some kind of a trade or start calling things out on Twitter again live.

section_title: UNFAVORABLE TRADING CONDITIONS
content:
On the 4-hour chart, let's try to get through this a little bit because we're running out of time here. This is
really not a market that is, in my opinion, symmetrical, meaning that the price swings are really obvious;
they're running for clear liquidity inefficiencies and then going to another obvious one. It's just doing very
much the same thing we saw in the Dollar Index: just marking time, small ranges, a lot of back and forth
price action. Can I trade that? Yes. Can some of my students? Can they trade it on the lower time frames?
Yes, but these are the conditions that I try to teach new students to avoid because there's better markets
and better market conditions to trade. You shouldn't be trying to risk anything in these types of conditions
because you don't have the experience to be able to navigate it well. You'll think that the moves are going
to continue, and they won't; they'll pull back in the range and retrace, either stop you out or scare you if you
don't use a stop loss, and then you start drawing down your account. If you read the comments on Twitter,
there's a number of people that got beat up last week because they still tried to do something. I wasn't
recommending but I was suggesting I wasn't interested in taking anything last week going into the week
unless it did specific things. It didn't do it.

section_title: SELL-SIDE LIQUIDITY OBJECTIVES
content:
On the 60-minute chart, again, this is ugly price action in here; I don't like that. I don't like the fact that they
left this even. There are some gaps down here, and there's liquidity here, so sell side here, sell side here,
inefficiency down here. I like the fact that we've taken this buy side, so we marked that out real quick. I don't
want to get too wrapped up in the annotations, but we ran it here one more time in here. Again, I'd like to
see a gap opening lower, come back, fill the gap, trade for the sell side here, and trade for the sell side
here. That's what I'd like to see going into London tonight, going into Monday's trading. That's the extent of
what I have right now going forward on Euro.

section_title: VALUE GAP
content:
Cable or Pound Dollar, this is our weekly chart here. This one here, just not all that excited about it, but
longer term, I'd like to see it trade down here. That's what I was mentioning last week: sell side resides in
here. We have an inefficiency with an order block, so if we get animated on the downside and continuation
up on the Dollar Index, again, this is where I'm looking down here. We do have a Sell-Side Imbalance Buy
- Side Efficiency (SISI) in the form of this candle's low, this candle's high, that single down-closed candle. We
trade up in the consequent encroachment here two weeks ago, and then we had not an inside range; we
had a lower low on the week than the previous week, but we found support in this area down here. I
mentioned that that would act
- and we'll see it on the daily chart now
- I'd like to see it get below that and
act as an inversion fair value gap. I think I'm correct in saying it that way, but don't take my word for it; go
back and listen to last Sunday's live stream, it's part of the 2023 mentorship commentary. So this
inefficiency we went through it, went through it again here, and I want to see it go down below it and then
treat it as resistance and then move lower into those weekly objectives down here. That's what I'm looking
at for the daily time frame for Pound Dollar.

section_title: UGLY PRICE ACTION AND EURO PREFERENCE
content:
On the 4-hour chart real quick, we've taken sell side here. The bodies were respecting that fair value gap.
This is really ugly price action. I really would not touch Cable here. It would have to do something really
animated to the downside before I would participate in this one. So just know that while I have co-signed
and said what I'd like to see it do directionally and where it would draw to for my analysis, I wouldn't touch
this one. I would be more inclined to trade the Euro if the Dollar Index were to resume going higher this
week. That might change in the beginning of the week if I start seeing something different or if I see
something at the opening here in a couple minutes, it might change that opinion. But right now, I would
favor Euro over Cable for the week. And you can just see, just really sloppy, really not a good week at all to
be trading Cable if you don't know what you're doing.

section_title: INDEX FUTURES (ES): QUARTERLY SHIFT TARGET AND SELL-SIDE LIQUIDITY
content:
Now let's get into Index Futures because that's where we're at really in this mentorship this year. I know
some of you don't like that because you want me to do just Forex, but you got to get what you can get,
right? Everything's the same, price action is the same regardless of what asset class. So here's the
quarterly shift target I was talking about months ago. We were up here trading, we broke down, and then
last week we had a really nice sell-off in here. Obviously, there are relative equal lows there, so there's sell
side below that. And then we have the consequent encroachment of this range right here. So we have
midpoint, which is 14431 and 3/4. I'd like that level, I'd like to see it trade down in there. But initially, we got
to see how and if we get below these relative equal lows; there's sell side resting below, I mean sell stops.
So it looks like it's in a hurry to get down there. It resumed off of this Sell-Side Imbalance Buy-Side
Efficiency (SIBI) between this candle's low, this candle's high. It traded up into that there, and then rejected
that, overshot the consequent encroachment on this wick, but it went into the upper quadrant. We'll take a
look at that real quick.

section_title: BEARISH OUTLOOK AND GAP LOWER SCENARIO
content:
It just fell short of that. I thought for sure it looked like it hit that but got real close to it. It went to mean
consequent correction of this inefficiency of the wick. We could treat that the same way as any other gap,
traded there, and then aggressively moved lower. So I want to see it gap lower than when we closed, and
then rush underneath these lows, accelerate down into here. That's what I want to see. It doesn't mean it's
going to happen, but that's what I would like to see happen. And it's not meant for you to be taking this as
trade advice. The best thing that can happen is for me to get it wrong and you can see how I would
navigate the rest of the week with that insight. So again, sell side resting below here, got real close into it
here, makes perfect sense for it to be gapping down there below it. And then if it does gap below that and
we start to come back up, we'd have to see it respect this inefficiency right in here, it would have to stay
below.

section_title: KEY RESISTANCE LEVELS AND BREAKAWAY GAP
content:
Specifically that low, but it can spike up to consequent encroachment of this wick. I would like to see it stop
there because in this case it would be consequent encroachment of this fair value gap. Then we have the
consequent encroachment of this wick, and I wouldn't even look at this volume balance because I want to
see this volume balance act as a breakaway gap. So I want to see it gap open lower and show willingness,
I want to keep going, moving lower. Again, sell side in here, just really ugly price action all through here,
and finally broke down, gave a nice continuation with a market maker sell model which I'll amplify on the 4
- hour chart of NASDAQ because that's the one I operated on. And then we have a bearish order block in
here. The market again reaching for sell side. On the 60-minute chart, sell side imbalance buy efficiency
here, trade up into the breaker which is high, low, higher high, there's the low in the middle, trades up into it
here, leaves a small portion open. That's exceedingly bearish. I'd like to see that. And then we had that nice
sell off in the afternoon on Friday, so we'll be looking for that to continue.

section_title: QUARTERLY SHIFT OBJECTIVE AND INEFFICIENCY
content:
Now finally, making our way over to NASDAQ. I won't be talking about gold, but I will comment on it
tomorrow during the New York session on Twitter, so if you're interested in knowing all the business there,
you obviously see that there. Weekly again, down here is my quarterly shift objective. People were getting
real excited last previous week, 'Oh, it's going to keep going higher!' And then we got this. So it's
unfortunate for the folks that like to fade me, but the consequent encroachment of this Buy-Side Imbalance
Sell-Side Efficiency (BISI) and sell side here, I expect to see it draw in. I'd like to see it draw here this week,
but again, that's what I want, doesn't mean it's going to have to do it. But we did work up into this old
inefficiency that's shaded here, so we traded up into that. And then here is this wick there; we got above
consequent encroachment and just fell short of the upper quadrant which it could have spiked to, would
have been just outside that fair value gap in the form of a BISI, so that would have been an allowance for
that.

section_title: SELL-SIDE LIQUIDITY AND OPENING SCENARIOS
content:
On the daily chart, obvious sell side resting down here. Again, I want to see this gap open below here;
that's what I'd like to see. I want to see it open right below that blue line right there. If it opens relatively
unchanged, then that means we might have to roll up here a little bit and then resume lower. That's how I
expect that. How far up into this, I would use this consequent encroachment there. So 757, that would be
on that level. So if we open relatively unchanged or above the close, then I would look for it to trade here
and then resume lower. That's how I treat that. But I'm preferring to see opening below here to take it right
into that liquidity for the folks that didn't get a chance to hold over the weekend. They would be upset, like I
would have been at 20 years old, not holding over the weekend because I would have missed out on that
really profitable exit. But 50,000, 51,000 is pretty good for a couple hours.
NQ 4
- HOUR CHART:

section_title: MARKET MAKER SELL MODEL (MMSM) STRUCTURE
content:
Looking at the 4-hour chart here, this is a market maker sell model. So original consolidation, I walked you
through this on the Twitter space, came back, touched, and revisited the original consolidation here. So it's
accumulation, reaccumulation, smart money reversal, low risk sell, first stage distribution, second stage
redistribution. But right in here, I saw that it was not willing or hasn't yet gone below the original
consolidation. Sell side is, so in about a minute and a half it's going to open up. Right in here, inside this
inefficiency, that's what I was utilizing. So I would have liked to have seen it trade back up into this
inefficiency and treat that as an area to use as an inversion fair value gap, but it was unwilling to get there.

section_title: BEARISH BREAKER AND LIQUIDITY PREFERENCE
content:
When we dropped down to a 60-minute chart, in here we're trading up in here, and we failed to get into that
4-hour old Buy-Side Imbalance Sell-Side Efficiency (BISI) that I wanted to see act as an inversion fair value
gap. It never got there. And even though it has relative equal lows here, I'm not worried about that because
the liquidity below here, which is based on that larger 4-hour area of liquidity here, that to me is a better
draw than this will be. And it's going to be, in my opinion, in a hurry to get down below these lows over here
more so than this to come back up here to get to that, because it's already done a lot of give and take back
and forth. So I favored that in the 15-minute time frame.

section_title: NASDAQ OPENING ANALYSIS AND GEOPOLITICAL IMPACT
content:
You can see that is what I worked on. Oh, you got a slight open gap higher, so that's not what I wanted to
see. And in fact, I actually did a better job of managing the trade on Friday by closing it by trailing the stop
loss real real tight and letting it take me out of the marketplace. This is again what I was kind of opening up
with the commentary. I didn't see anything that was so troubling or worrisome that would have caused a lot
of excitement, so I wanted to see how we opened because there wasn't anything new. All of it's bad. I gotta
be careful how I say this, because I'm sure I'm going to hurt somebody's feelings and it's not intended to be
that way, but nothing is grossly exaggerated like I'm expecting things to get worse over there. And because
of that, that's going to have reverberations in the marketplace. But because I didn't see anything on the
newswires, and nobody I know of that's living over there reported anything much more than what we've
already anticipated seeing, so there wasn't a larger acceleration to the downside. So do I subscribe to
going for a run higher? I'd like to see it shake off this opening gap, break lower, and still go down for the sell
side. That's what I'd like to see once it gets down in below here.

section_title: GEOPOLITICAL ESCALATION VS. NON-ESCALATION SCENARIOS
content:
Barring any kind of unexpected event that would cause a lot of people to get freaked out, it could trade
down below the sell here, and then if nothing increases in the excitement or turmoil that's going on over
there, and the saber rattling and the war stuff that's going on, it could take the sell side here and then go all
the way back up to here and reach for the buy side. That's what I would look for if we don't see escalation.
So if we see escalation in the wartime things that are going on over in Israel and Gaza, then I expect it to
resume going lower. If we don't see escalation
- I gotta make sure I said that right because I'm doing it live
- no escalation, we'll eventually run back up into this the first two days of the week. If we escalate, this will
stay in place and we'll just keep going lower. Hopefully that kind of makes a little bit sense. Because of all
the things that are going on, I take a lot of consideration for what's going on. Anything war related, anything
that is, you know, violence related, those are a different market condition entirely, versus, you know, an
interest rate expectation or bad economy things. That's something that, you know, I'm not too concerned
about that. I'll trade ahead of things and not be afraid of, you know, the underlying risks with it. But these
types of events where you don't know how bad things can just pop off and do something unexpected, and
it'll happen when you're not expecting it in a time of the day when it wouldn't usually move big, this is
usually how it happens. So that's why I've been counseling all of you that are listening to me and hang on
like every word I say: you have to be really really careful right now. Try not to be getting home runs. Trade
with the smallest leverage, use a stop loss.

section_title: NQ: BEARISH BREAKER AND FRIDAY'S TRADE MANAGEMENT
content:
In here, you can see we have this high, that low, and that high that makes this down closed candle my
bearish breaker right here. Because all of this movement hadn't yet taken the sell side which I was aiming
for, I still believe it's still a viable objective. I've not abandoned that even though we had this really
uneventful opening price here. We traded right back up into it here. You can see that candle has my two
entries here right in here. So it went up, hit it, came back off and I got filled there, and then I added more in
here. And ultimately, you watched me get stopped with the trailed stop loss right there. So I was leaving it
there in case we got this big runoff acceleration down in the sell side on Friday, which admittedly I was kind
of expecting that but it just didn't come to fruition. That's okay, but that's a nice little run. Nothing else to
speak of for the week but using the information and the logic based on that 4-hour market maker sell model.
CONCLUSION:

section_title: LAST MODEL AND FINAL TRADING ADVICE
content:
Which incidentally is my last model for this YouTube channel. I will be teaching it, I will be sharing it on
October 31st at 9:00 PM New York local time. So that will be the last educational material that will close out
my 2023 mentorship on the YouTube channel. It'll close out all the instructional portion. It doesn't mean I
won't do live streams, it doesn't mean I won't do a market review between now and November 10th. It just
means that there won't be any formalized, structured lectures or teachings after that one. It kind of like
closes this chapter in Inner Circle Trader for YouTube and Twitter. So that will be it for today. Hopefully you
found this insightful. I want you to be careful going into this week. Try not to do too much. Pick your shots,
be very very nimble, do not risk a lot, and don't over leverage. And until I talk to you tomorrow, Lord willing,
be safe.
